questions.prompt: What should be the primary navigation model after combining `Workspace`, `Sessions`, and `Settings`?

options.text: Single unified workbench (sidebar tree + main panel context)
Keep separate routes but hide them behind one combined sidebar entry
Top-level tabs inside one page (Workspace/Sessions/Settings)
Minimal change: keep current nav items, just add cross-links

questions.prompt: In v1, which settings should be treated as workspace-scoped vs app-global?

options.text: Everything shown as workspace-scoped; no dedicated global settings page
Split: tiny app-global prefs + workspace-scoped operational settings
Keep current Settings page as-is (global) but reachable from workbench
Defer: keep existing split until later

questions.prompt: For the sidebar workspace list, how should multiple workspaces behave?

options.text: Show recent workspaces; any can expand to show sessions (lazy-load on expand)
Show only the active workspace; workspace switching via dropdown/picker
Accordion: list recent workspaces but only one expanded at a time
Only show workspaces inside a modal picker (not persistent)

answers.notes: I think everything starts off as expanded. And switching between them would be a lot like switching between chats in the chatGPT interface.

questions.prompt: What should the `Apply to all modes` control do for run defaults?

options.text: Apply model + thinking level from current mode to all roles
Apply model only (leave thinking level unchanged)
Apply only to the three main modes (planning/implementation/new work)
Provide two actions: `Apply model` and `Apply model + thinking`
Do nothing in UI; rely on users editing a JSON file

questions.prompt: Which Git capabilities are required in the first usable iteration?

options.text: Panel with fetch/pull/push + branch switch/create + merge; show stdout/stderr; no conflict UI
Only fetch/pull/push and current-branch status (no branch ops)
Full git client UI (staging, per-file actions, conflict resolution)
Freeform command box to run arbitrary git commands

questions.prompt: What default safety behavior should `Pull` use?

options.text: Default to `pull --ff-only` to avoid implicit merge commits
Use plain `git pull` (whatever user config does)
Always `pull --rebase`
Always prompt the user to choose the pull strategy each time

questions.prompt: How should `Open in VS Code` work cross-platform?

options.text: Try `code` CLI first; fallback to OS open; show clear error if neither works
Require `code` CLI; show setup instructions otherwise
Never use `code`; always OS open only
User-configurable command path in settings

questions.prompt: When a session is selected, what should happen to the layout?

options.text: Session becomes main panel content; sidebar stays; workspace edit uses a flyout side sheet
Keep the current session flyout overlay; just move the session list into sidebar
Open sessions in a new window
Session replaces the entire window (no sidebar while viewing a session)

questions.prompt: How should the run feed present the final response?

options.text: Always render final response as full markdown; keep event details collapsible
Show final response only; hide the underlying event stream entirely
Keep current event stream UI but auto-expand final response details
Render final response only as plain text (no markdown formatting)

questions.prompt: How should session activity indicators classify the type of work in progress?

options.text: Spinner + icon derived from run role and UI action (Q&A/implement/testing/scripts/follow-up/generic)
Spinner only (no type icon)
Only indicate potentially code-modifying work (implementation/testing)
Show a numeric progress indicator (based on event count) instead of type

questions.prompt: Should the per-workspace `House Style` markdown be display-only, or also injected into Codex prompts?

options.text: Display-only (UI reference only)
Inject into planning prompts only
Inject into planning + implementation prompts
Inject into all runs (planning/implementation/testing/generic)
User-toggle per run (checkbox)

questions.prompt: The new bottom composer should append to the current session. What should it target by default?

options.text: Always the planning thread (Q&A)
Always the implementation thread
Default to the current tab (planning vs implementation), with a dropdown to override
Always start a new generic run (no thread continuation)
Keep per-tab inputs only (no bottom composer)

questions.prompt: When you expand a non-active workspace in the sidebar, should that also activate/switch to it?

options.text: Yes — expanding also activates it (chat-like switching)
No — expanding is passive; activating requires a separate click/action
Allow expanding multiple workspaces without switching the active one (peek sessions)
Only one workspace at a time; switch via picker (no sidebar list)

questions.prompt: For the new chat-like experience, should the session have one unified timeline across all modes, or separate timelines per mode?

options.text: Separate timelines per mode (Planning / Implementation / Testing)
One unified timeline, with a mode filter (chips/tabs)
One unified timeline always showing everything (no filter)
Keep current mode tabs, but only make planning/testing chat-like

questions.prompt: You requested that Q&A/testing rounds and their actions live as controls in the history. How literal should that be?

options.text: All primary actions are inline control cards in the history (chat-like)
Actions stay in a bottom toolbar near the composer (history is read-only)
Both: inline control cards + a small bottom toolbar for quick access
Keep actions where they are today; only move the composer/config

questions.prompt: How should Q&A rounds appear in the planning history?

options.text: Round cards show questions + chosen answers; expand to edit in place
Round cards are summary-only; editing opens a modal
Only the latest round is editable; prior rounds are read-only
Keep the current Q&A editor unchanged; add a separate history view

questions.prompt: What should the bottom “configuration around everything” look like in the session view?

options.text: Always-visible compact config row above the composer (profile/model/thinking/etc)
Config hidden behind a settings button that expands inline
Config stays at the top; only the composer moves to the bottom
Config is per-action card only (each history control has its own config)

questions.prompt: Should plan/test documents be rendered inline as part of the chat-like history (collapsible), or kept as separate dedicated views?

options.text: Render inline as collapsible history cards (with an “open file” affordance)
Keep as separate views/tabs; history only shows rounds/runs
Inline preview only (first ~N lines), full doc always opened separately
No inline rendering; only show links to open the files

notes:
- Sidebar activity: spinner only (remove work-type icon).
- Sidebar workspace settings: no settings icon in the sidebar; use the workspace header settings action.
- Sidebar layout: place “New workspace” control above/below search as its own row (not squeezed inline).
- Main panel: no “Workbench” card; show a simple empty state when no session is selected.
